summary.text: Extension methods for adding Bitwarden Secrets Manager resources.

members.name: AddBitwardenSecretManager
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<BitwardenSecretManagerResource> BitwardenSecretManagerExtensions.AddBitwardenSecretManager(this IDistributedApplicationBuilder builder, string name, IResourceBuilder<ParameterResource> projectNameOrId, IResourceBuilder<ParameterResource> organizationId, IResourceBuilder<ParameterResource> accessToken)

summary.text: Adds a Bitwarden Secrets Manager resource. The
parameter resolves to either a project name (creates or finds by name) or a project identifier GUID (adopts the existing project by ID).

accessToken.text: The access token parameter used to manage the Bitwarden project and managed secrets.

organizationId.text: The parameter that resolves to the Bitwarden organization identifier.

projectNameOrId.text: The parameter that resolves to the Bitwarden project name or project identifier (GUID).

summary.text: Adds a managed Bitwarden secret whose local and remote names are the same. The secret value is resolved from configuration key
Parameters:{parentName}-{name}

name.text: The Aspire resource name and Bitwarden secret name.

members.name: AddSecret
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<BitwardenSecretResource> BitwardenSecretManagerExtensions.AddSecret(this IResourceBuilder<BitwardenSecretManagerResource> builder, string name, string remoteName)

summary.text: Adds a managed Bitwarden secret with distinct Aspire and remote names. The secret value is resolved from configuration key
Parameters:{parentName}-{name}
Returns an
that resolves to the Bitwarden secret identifier. Pass it to
WithEnvironment
to inject the secret ID as an environment variable. The app then uses the Bitwarden SDK to fetch the secret value at runtime.

returns.text: An expression value that resolves to the Bitwarden secret identifier.

members.name: GetSecret
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<BitwardenSecretResource> BitwardenSecretManagerExtensions.GetSecret(this IResourceBuilder<BitwardenSecretManagerResource> builder, string name)

summary.text: Gets or creates a Bitwarden secret reference whose Aspire and remote names are the same. The secret must already exist in Bitwarden; use
if Aspire should own and write the secret value.

returns.text: A resource builder for the secret reference.

name.text: The Aspire resource name and Bitwarden secret name.

members.name: GetSecret

summary.text: Gets or creates a Bitwarden secret reference with distinct Aspire and remote names. The secret must already exist in Bitwarden; use
if Aspire should own and write the secret value.

returns.text: A resource builder for the secret reference.

members.name: GetSecret
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<BitwardenSecretResource> BitwardenSecretManagerExtensions.GetSecret(this IResourceBuilder<BitwardenSecretManagerResource> builder, string name, Guid secretId)

summary.text: Gets or creates a Bitwarden secret reference by secret identifier. Use this when multiple secrets share the same name and the identifier is the only unambiguous key. The secret must already exist in Bitwarden; use
if Aspire should own and write the secret value.

returns.text: A resource builder for the secret reference.

summary.text: Overrides the Bitwarden API URL.
Overrides the Bitwarden API URL using a parameter.

apiUrl.text: The parameter that resolves to the absolute Bitwarden API URL.

summary.text: Overrides the Bitwarden API URL using an external service resource. The Bitwarden resource will wait for the external service before authenticating.

summary.text: Overrides the Bitwarden API URL using an endpoint from another resource in the Aspire model.

endpoint.text: The endpoint reference for the Bitwarden API.

summary.text: Overrides the AppHost auth cache directory (Bitwarden SDK auth session used by the AppHost reconciler). Defaults to the Aspire store when not set. Override to reuse a cached auth session across CI runs. To configure the auth cache directory inside the deployed app, use

authCacheDirectory.text: The auth cache directory on the AppHost, relative to the Aspire store when not rooted.

summary.text: Overrides the AppHost cache file path (integration bookkeeping: Bitwarden project ID, secret ID mappings). Defaults to
.bitwarden/{resourceName}.{environment}.json
relative to the AppHost directory. Override to share the cache across multiple AppHost projects, or to store it in a CI cache directory.

cacheFile.text: The cache file path, relative to the AppHost directory when not rooted.
